SPY.notes: V-reversal continuation but fading from midday highs of $657.92. Pulled back ~$3 from 12:00 report level of $656. Volume 29% of average — low liquidity afternoon ahead of Good Friday. Consolidating at gamma flip zone $654-656.

QQQ.notes: Holding morning recovery gains. Outperforming SPY on percentage basis. Day range narrowing — consolidation mode. Tech stabilizing after early TSLA drag.

IWM.notes: Faded from morning high of $252.44. Pre-market showed $245.59 (-1.6%). Recovery less convincing than SPY/QQQ — small caps giving back relative outperformance from 12:00 snapshot.

vix.notes: VIX rising into afternoon — up 4.2% vs earlier decline. Reversal from morning vol crush. Suggests uncertainty returning ahead of weekend/Good Friday closure. VIX at 25.57 is the HIGH regime (20-30). Midday report from StockMarketWatch confirmed VIX climbed 2.06% to 25.77.

fear_greed.label: Extreme Fear
fear_greed.notes: Unchanged from 12:00 snapshot. Extreme Fear persists — 5th+ consecutive day in single-digit/teens territory. Powerful contrarian signal but muted by active Iran war and Hormuz blockade deadline April 6.

oil.notes: WTI pulled back from $111.60 highs to $105.57. Brent at $108.90. Oil intraday range $97.50-$111.60 is extraordinary ($14 swing). Settling into mid-$105 range. Still up 6% on day. April 6 Hormuz blockade deadline and OPEC April 5 meeting are critical.

treasury.notes: 10-year yield edged down to 4.30% from intraday high of 4.38%. Flight to safety keeping yields suppressed despite inflationary oil surge.

sectors.laggards: Consumer Discretionary (Sherwin-Williams -3.81%, Nike -2.98%, Home Depot -2.66%), Tech (TSLA -4%, NVDA -2%, META -2%)

gold.notes: Gold plunging -2.6% as investors liquidate safe havens to chase energy. GLD down -2.22%.

sectors.notes: Clear energy vs everything rotation. Only energy sector green. Consumer discretionary worst performer.

options_flow.notes: Extreme put buying — most bearish options positioning in weeks. SPY OI P/C ratio near 2.0. Historically contrarian bullish when this extreme, but war catalyst makes contrarian unreliable.

geopolitical.headline: Trump vows to 'hit Iran hard' for 2-3 more weeks, no plan to reopen Hormuz
geopolitical.catalyst: Iran/Oman working on Hormuz shipping protocol — drove morning V-reversal
geopolitical.key_dates: April 5 OPEC meeting, April 6 Hormuz blockade deadline
geopolitical.risk: Binary outcome: diplomacy succeeds (oil crashes, equities rally) or fails (oil $120+, equities capitulate)
